name: Gratin of Walleye
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and pat the fillets dry, then dice into 1-inch squares. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Wash, then dry the green peppers and roast them under the broiler, turning as the skin blackens.
3. Place the peppers in a paper bag, seal by twisting the top, and set aside for about 5 minutes. Remove and, using your finger, push and pinch off most of the blackened skin. Cut in half, remove the seeds and stems, and slice into thin strips.
4. Heat oil in a large skillet and sauté garlic until translucent. Add fillet squares from the cutting board and cook on high heat, stirring constantly for about 5 minutes.
5. Off heat, add peppers, parsley, capers, and half the bread crumbs mixed with thyme. Mix thoroughly.
6. Spoon into baking dish and sprinkle remaining crumb-thyme mixture over all. Place under the broiler for a minute or two to brown.
7. Serve with tossed salad and baked potatoes.
